J1.basis: Root cause: this is a mid-pivot site. The product changed from "InboxZap" (bulk email cleanup) to "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant)... GSC confirms it: the brand term "inboxzap" still ranks ~#4, but every real-intent query from the old product... is stuck at position 40–64 with near-zero clicks
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, sources it three ways (live page = Lumina, GSC = legacy inbox intent, code = stale), and frames every recommendation around the new positioning, including refusing to chase legacy terms.

J2.basis: GSC shows 12 indexed legacy post URLs (`/blog/post-1`…`post-12`, up to 3,900 impressions)... Added 301 redirects to `/blog` (their content was InboxZap-era and has no Lumina equivalent — redirecting preserves link equity instead of leaving dead ends)
J2.rationale: It grounds the decision in a real (aggregate) GSC figure and gives a disposition with reasoning, but applies a single blanket 301-to-index to all 12 rather than walking them asset-by-asset or in data-justified tiers with individual impressions/positions.

J3.basis: chasing "bulk delete emails" traffic makes no sense for a meeting-assistant product... redirecting preserves link equity instead of leaving dead ends... target new keywords aligned to what Lumina actually does
J3.rationale: Explicitly rejects both extremes — it won't rank legacy inbox content, but it 301-redirects to preserve equity rather than torching it, while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine; the transition bridge is real but thin (redirect-all-to-index).

J1.basis: Root cause: this is a mid-pivot site. The product changed from "InboxZap" to "Lumina"... GSC confirms it: the brand term "inboxzap" still ranks ~#4, but every real-intent query from the old product... is stuck at position 40–64
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live site as the meeting-assistant product and the GSC inbox-cleanup queries as legacy demand, and frames every fix and the growth plan around that reality.

J2.basis: GSC shows 12 indexed legacy post URLs (`/blog/post-1`…`post-12`, up to 3,900 impressions)... Added 301 redirects to `/blog` (their content was InboxZap-era and has no Lumina equivalent...)
J2.rationale: Assigns a real, reasoned disposition (blanket 301 to the index) and cites aggregate impressions/positions, but treats all 12 posts identically rather than walking them asset-by-asset or in data-justified tiers — no harvest of any individually salvageable high-impression post.

J3.basis: chasing "bulk delete emails" traffic makes no sense for a meeting-assistant product... redirect to the blog index instead of leaving them as 404s... target new keywords aligned to what Lumina actually does
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes — declines to chase inbox traffic yet redirects rather than torching legacy equity, and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine; docked slightly because redirecting everything to a bare index is a weak equity-preservation move (prone to soft-404 treatment) with no per-asset bridge to a relevant successor.

J1.basis: this is a mid-pivot site. The product changed from "InboxZap" (bulk email cleanup) to "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant), but the code was never fully updated. GSC confirms it... every real-intent query from the old product... is stuck at position 40–64 with near-zero clicks
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot, correctly reads GSC as legacy demand vs. the live meeting-assistant product, and frames every fix and the growth recommendation around that reality.

J2.basis: GSC shows 12 indexed legacy post URLs (/blog/post-1…post-12, up to 3,900 impressions)... Added 301 redirects to /blog (their content was InboxZap-era and has no Lumina equivalent)
J2.rationale: It gives a real disposition (blanket 301 to /blog) with a rationale and cites an aggregate impressions figure, but treats all 12 identically rather than walking them asset-by-asset or in data-justified tiers — no per-post impressions/positions and it forecloses harvesting the higher-value ones without checking them individually.

J3.basis: chasing "bulk delete emails" traffic makes no sense for a meeting-assistant product... redirect to the blog index instead of leaving them as 404s... target new keywords aligned to what Lumina actually does
J3.rationale: Clearly avoids both failure modes — rejects chasing dead inbox intent while preserving link equity via 301s rather than torching it, and points growth at the meeting-assistant cluster — but the transition/bridge is thin (single redirect target, no sequenced equity-consolidation plan) so it lands short of a fully articulated middle path.
